spec_target: A call to krealloc that returns NULL (indicating allocation failure)
spec_predicate: The original allocation passed to krealloc must be freed with kfree before returning, if krealloc returns NULL
reason: The analysis reveals that `krealloc` does not internally handle failure cases where it returns NULL. In all usage examples, when `krealloc` fails (returns NULL), the code immediately returns -ENOMEM without freeing the original allocation. This leaves the original memory unreleased, creating a memory leak. Callers must explicitly free the original allocation with `kfree` when `krealloc` returns NULL to prevent this resource leak.
